title: Campaign Monitor vs Moosend (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare Campaign Monitor vs Moosend. See pricing, features, ratings & which email marketing tool suits your business best.
h1: Campaign Monitor vs Moosend: Detailed Comparison
intro: Campaign Monitor and Moosend are both established email marketing platforms, but they serve different business needs. Campaign Monitor excels at multi-brand management for agencies, while Moosend offers exceptional value for small businesses with its freemium model and unlimited sending. Understanding their differences will help you choose the right tool for your marketing strategy.
choose_a_if: You manage multiple client accounts or brands and need dedicated multi-brand features
Your primary focus is enterprise-level email design and customer journey mapping
You require advanced transactional email capabilities for your business
choose_b_if: You're a startup or small business wanting to start free and scale affordably
You need unlimited email sending without worrying about per-contact costs
You want landing pages and subscription forms integrated into your email platform

faq.q: Is Campaign Monitor better than Moosend?
faq.a: Neither is objectively 'better'—it depends on your needs. Campaign Monitor excels for agencies managing multiple brands with sophisticated journey design, while Moosend offers superior value for small businesses with its free tier and unlimited sending. Moosend's higher customer ratings (4.7 on G2 vs 4.1) suggest broader user satisfaction overall.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, Campaign Monitor or Moosend?
faq.a: Moosend is significantly cheaper, especially for startups, with a free plan supporting up to 1,000 subscribers and paid plans starting at $9/month. Campaign Monitor has no free tier and starts at $11/month. For small businesses, Moosend's unlimited emails at lower price points make it the more economical choice.

faq.q: Can I switch from Moosend to Campaign Monitor?
faq.a: Yes, most email marketing platforms allow subscriber list exports, making migration feasible. Both tools support standard email formats and integrations. However, you may lose advanced automation workflows during transfer, so it's wise to document your setup in Moosend before switching to avoid data loss.
